hero.headline: A travel atlas that happens to be a word-search game.
hero.subhead: Word Search Atlas turns each puzzle into a destination, with globe routes, daily stops, collectible cosmetics, and local progress that stays on the device.

summary: This repo ships a word-search game with a web build, an iOS shell, a globe campaign map, daily routes, free play, and on-device progress storage.
audience: Players who want short, touch-friendly puzzle sessions with more structure than a random grid.
Travel-theme puzzle fans who like place-driven vocabulary and a map-based progression loop.
Offline players looking for a game that still works on flights, commutes, or weak connections.
coreProblems: Many word-search games feel generic and stateless from one round to the next.
Travel themes are often decorative instead of shaping the actual vocabulary and progression.
Offline support, local saves, and touch-first board controls are often unclear or inconsistent.

features.title: Campaign map
features.summary: The shipped UI centers progression around a globe and location list rather than a flat level picker.

features.title: Daily routes
features.summary: Daily puzzles and streak tracking are called out in the repo metadata, support copy, and main interface.

features.title: Free play and themes
features.summary: Players can switch between random boards and themed word pools without leaving the core puzzle loop.

features.title: Local progression
features.summary: XP, coins, unlocks, medals, and achievements are stored locally so the profile persists between sessions.

features.title: Touch-first controls
features.summary: The repo explicitly targets iPhone and iPad layouts and uses drag selection as the main interaction.

workflows.title: Five-minute phone session
workflows.summary: Open the current route, drag through a few words, and keep the same save state for the next break.

workflows.title: Offline travel play
workflows.summary: The project is designed so the core experience remains usable after install or load, with progress stored on-device.

workflows.title: Progress-first puzzle loop
workflows.summary: Campaign stops, daily streaks, coins, and cosmetic unlocks give the repo a longer arc than a single disposable board.

workflows.title: Open-ended board play
workflows.summary: Free play, resize controls, and restart options let the player use it as a straight word-search app when needed.

proofPoints.label: Build surface
proofPoints.note: The repo includes a browser entry point, manifest, service worker, and a Capacitor iOS project.

proofPoints.label: Save model
proofPoints.note: The privacy page states that progress, settings, and unlocks are stored on-device by default.

proofPoints.label: Modes in repo
proofPoints.note: Those modes appear in the shipped interface and supporting metadata.

proofPoints.label: Public support surface
proofPoints.note: Both pages exist in the repo and are linked from the store metadata.

faq.question: Is App Store availability confirmed here?
faq.answer: No. The repo includes App Store metadata and an iOS wrapper, but that does not prove a current public App Store listing. This export uses the live web build as the safest primary CTA.

faq.question: Does the project support offline play?
faq.answer: The privacy page says the app is designed to work offline, and the store metadata says the core modes work offline once installed. Progress is stored locally on the device.

faq.question: Where does player progress live?
faq.answer: Current repo documentation says progress, settings, cosmetics, and daily history are kept in local storage on-device. No account is required by default.

faq.question: Why are there no gameplay screenshots here?
faq.answer: No actual gameplay screenshots were committed to the repository. This package uses shipped iconography and repo-derived product art instead of inventing mockups.

screenshots.alt: Illustrated product preview with a route line, letter tiles, and a globe marker.
screenshots.caption: Repo-derived product art used in place of gameplay screenshots.

screenshots.alt: Word Search Atlas app icon with a word grid lens and globe magnifier.
screenshots.caption: Shipped app icon from the repository.

catalog.title: Word Search Atlas
catalog.blurb: A travel-themed word search built around globe routes, local progress, and quiet offline play.

repoArchetypeReasoning: The repo ships a playable website with a manifest, service worker, and a full iOS Capacitor shell.
Store metadata, support/privacy pages, and iOS assets indicate a public-facing puzzle app rather than an internal utility.
The main interaction surface is gameplay: campaign routes, daily puzzles, themed boards, coins, and unlockable cosmetics.
assumptions: The GitHub Pages marketing URL is the safest primary CTA because current App Store availability is not proven by the repo alone.
iPhone and iPad support are treated as intended platforms because the repo includes iOS packaging and store copy that names both.
No gameplay screenshots were committed, so the export uses branded art instead of fabricated device mockups.
missingButUsefulAssets: Captured gameplay screenshots from the showcase routes listed in store/screenshot-plan.md
A wide social preview image exported from the live build
An explicit public changelog or release note page
